learning_outcomes.description: Collaboratively evaluate business strategies using sustainability science frameworks and engaging with relevant stakeholders.
Propose innovative solutions to sustainability issues.
Develop a sustainability strategy that is adaptive and resilient in overcoming challenges.
Reflect on personal sustainability leadership, considering impact, relationships, ethics, and critical thinking.
Discuss sustainability initiatives to enlist support of relevant stakeholders.

assessments.task_description:
An individual reflective journal in which students document and analyse their personal growth as sustainability leaders. Students will submit 4 separate journal entries in weeks 3, 5, 7 & 9. Students should focus on 1 (one) critical insights per entry such as leadership experiences, challenges, ethical considerations, and values in practice. 

Graduate Attribute: GA6
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Team Project - Report, Presentation and Q&A
assessments.due_date:
Presentation During Class - 13/10/2026

Report 20/10/2026 1:00 pm

Presentations (Part B of the assessment) will be presented in the Week 11 seminar.

Reports (Part A of the assessment) are due Week 12 Tuesday at 1pm.
assessments.task_description:
Students work in teams to research, critique, and co-develop a sustainability strategy in collaboration with an industry partner. Projects are open-ended, allowing students to identify the focus area within the partner’s sustainability agenda. Teams are expected to conduct benchmarking, stakeholder consultation, and strategic analysis, culminating in a strategy and implementation plan. 

Graduate Attributes: GA2, GA3
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
26/10/2026

Reflective interviews will take place during the scheduled seminar time in week 13.
assessments.task_description:
A short (15 minutes) individual oral assessment where students reflect on their leadership development, stakeholder engagement skills, and capacity to communicate persuasively about sustainability challenges and strategies. 

Graduate Attribute: GA5
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Course Introduction + Peer Recap

Introduction to the capstone structure, assessment expectations, and project scope. Peer networking and review of foundational sustainability knowledge. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Course Introduction + Peer Recap Introduction to the capstone structure, assessment expectations, and project scope. Peer networking and review of foundational sustainability knowledge.

learning_activities.topic:
Leadership + Working with Community

Exploration of sustainability leadership models and strategies for building trust and influence across diverse stakeholder groups.

Learning outcomes: L01, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Leadership + Working with Community Exploration of sustainability leadership models and strategies for building trust and influence across diverse stakeholder groups.

learning_activities.topic:
Industry Partner – Top 5 Sustainability Challenges

Guest session with industry partner presenting their current sustainability context and challenges. Student teams select or refine project themes.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Industry Partner – Top 5 Sustainability Challenges Guest session with industry partner presenting their current sustainability context and challenges. Student teams select or refine project themes.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical – Consultation + Engagement (Interviewing, Designing Questions)

Workshop on effective consultation techniques and stakeholder engagement. Teams design interview guides and outreach strategies. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical – Consultation + Engagement (Interviewing, Designing Questions) Workshop on effective consultation techniques and stakeholder engagement. Teams design interview guides and outreach strategies.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical – Data Collection + Management

Practical strategies for data sourcing, analysis, and visualisation. Teams begin collecting and organising research materials. 

Learning outcomes: L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical – Data Collection + Management Practical strategies for data sourcing, analysis, and visualisation. Teams begin collecting and organising research materials.

learning_activities.topic:
Progress Update Presentations

Each team presents a brief progress update, receives feedback from peers and teaching staff. Focus on refining scope and direction. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Progress Update Presentations Each team presents a brief progress update, receives feedback from peers and teaching staff. Focus on refining scope and direction.

learning_activities.topic:
Leadership Struggles

Reflective session exploring tensions in sustainability leadership—power, resistance, and value conflicts.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Leadership Struggles Reflective session exploring tensions in sustainability leadership—power, resistance, and value conflicts.

learning_activities.topic:
Industry Check-in (Feedback Session)

Midpoint feedback from industry partner. Teams present insights and preliminary directions, and refine based on input. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Industry Check-in (Feedback Session) Midpoint feedback from industry partner. Teams present insights and preliminary directions, and refine based on input.

learning_activities.topic:

Identify and analyse political, institutional, and cultural barriers to sustainability implementation. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical – Facing Resistance / Systemic Barriers Identify and analyse political, institutional, and cultural barriers to sustainability implementation.

Structured support for finalising assessment deliverables. Teams outline report structure, assign roles, and prepare for presentations.

learning_activities.topic_description: Assessment Development and Team Planning Structured support for finalising assessment deliverables. Teams outline report structure, assign roles, and prepare for presentations.

Final team presentations to industry partner and peers followed by Q&A. Emphasis on clarity, feasibility, and impact of proposed strategies.

learning_activities.topic_description: Industry Presentations Final team presentations to industry partner and peers followed by Q&A. Emphasis on clarity, feasibility, and impact of proposed strategies.
